---
title: "Mathias Abitbol"
image: images/portrait.jpeg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - text: CV
      href: files/CV.pdf
    - text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=iJlQGsEAAAAJ&hl=en
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0006-5006-4514
    - text: IDEAS/RePEc
      href: https://econpapers.repec.org/RAS/pab605.htm
    - text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mathias-abitbol-6a48761ba/
---

I am an economic researcher affiliated to the Ecole normale supérieure - PSL. My research focuses on the economics of the energy transition, at the intersection of environmental and energy economics, technological change, and corporate transformation.

I was trained at the École normale supérieure, the Paris School of Economics, and HEC Paris. I worked on the EU Carbon Markets at the French Ministry of Economy, on residential energy efficiency and energy taxation at EDF, and on fossil-energy markets and stranded assets at Mines Paris.

I am also the cofounder and president of [Daniel Cohen Conferences](https://laconferencedc.fr/). We organize high-level seminars gathering economists and public servants, and we interview policymakers and practitioners. 

## Research interests

Environmental and energy economics · Economics of
innovation and AI · Industrial decarbonization · Public Policy · Corporate
transformation

[See my research projects](research.qmd)

## Research Statement 

I am particularly interested in how regulation, finance, and new technologies affect firms'
energy use, production decisions, and decarbonization strategies. My work combines applied empirical research with close engagement with
public institutions and firms. I study how regulation, taxation, finance,
and new technologies shape firms' energy use and decarbonization strategies.


## Contact

Institution: Ecole normale supérieure - PSL

Department of Economics

Email: mathias.abitbol[at]ens[dot]fr
